title: NVIDIA BlueField-4 & ICMS: The New AI Storage Tier
description: How NVIDIA's BlueField-4 DPU and the Inference Context Memory Storage platform solve the KV cache bottleneck for agentic AI at scale.

nvidiaDPU.prompt: NVIDIA BlueField-4 data processing unit DPU chip, ultra-realistic photorealistic rendering, dark background, blue glowing circuit traces, futuristic hardware close-up, professional product photography style

aiFactory.prompt: Massive modern AI data center with rows of server racks, blue LED lighting, dark atmospheric environment, high-tech infrastructure, cinematic wide angle photography

beats.text: AI is revolutionizing the entire computing stack — and now, storage. Jensen Huang said that at CES 2026, and he meant it literally. As AI agents grow to trillion-parameter models reasoning over millions of tokens, the data center has hit a structural wall — and the breaking point is memory.
beats.speaker: Presenter

slide.quote: AI is revolutionizing the entire computing stack — and now, storage.
slide.author: Jensen Huang

beats.text: Every inference run generates a Key-Value cache: a scratchpad of attention states that must be read and written at extreme speed. GPU HBM is fast enough, but there's never enough of it — KV cache is competing directly with model weights. Conventional storage is cheap and spacious, but it was designed for durable enterprise records, not ephemeral, recomputable context that's stale in seconds.
beats.speaker: Presenter

content.value: The KV Cache Has No Good Home
Every inference run creates ephemeral attention state — too large for GPU memory, too transient for enterprise storage.

items: Trillions of params leave no room
Wrong access patterns for KV workloads

content.title: The Bottleneck

beats.text: NVIDIA's answer is a new storage tier called G3.5 — a flash-based intermediate layer that sits between GPU HBM and conventional storage. The key insight is that KV cache is ephemeral and recomputable. G3.5 strips away every enterprise storage overhead: no redundancy, no durability guarantees, no consistency protocols for long-lived records. Purpose-built for inference context means purpose-built for speed.
beats.speaker: Presenter

content.value: A New Flash-Based Inference Memory Tier

items: Positioned between GPU HBM and conventional storage
Powered by {primary:BlueField-4} DPU with NVMe/NVMe-oF + KV extensions
{success:Unlocks efficiency impossible with either existing tier}

content.title: Memory Hierarchy — G3.5 Fills the Gap

beats.text: Powering this new tier is the BlueField-4 data processor. It packs 800 gigabits per second of connectivity, a 64-core NVIDIA Grace CPU, and ConnectX-9 SuperNICs into a single platform — delivering six times the compute of BlueField-3 and supporting AI factories four times larger. It speaks NVMe and NVMe-oF with KV extensions natively, with zero-trust hardware security at line rate.
beats.speaker: Presenter

content.text: "The processor powering the OS of AI factories"

rows: Zero-trust · hardware encryption at line rate

beats.text: The Inference Context Memory Storage platform built on BlueField-4 delivers five times higher tokens per second and five times greater power efficiency compared to traditional storage approaches. A single GPU pod can share petabyte-scale KV cache over RDMA-accelerated Spectrum-X Ethernet. Reliable KV block prestaging eliminates decoder stalls, keeping GPUs computing rather than waiting.
beats.speaker: Presenter

items: Shared KV cache at {success:petabyte-scale} per GPU pod
KV block prestaging eliminates decoder stalls
Eliminates {danger:GPU idle cycles} caused by KV cache misses
Purpose-built ephemeral handling — no enterprise overhead
Direct TCO impact at hyperscaler rack scale

content.label: Tokens per second vs traditional storage
Shared KV cache per GPU pod

beats.text: The software stack coordinates four components. NVIDIA Dynamo orchestrates inference and manages KV block placement. DOCA provides the communication and storage interfaces as containerized microservices. Grove handles topology-aware workload placement across the cluster. And NIXL — the NVIDIA Inference Transfer Library — moves context fluidly across memory tiers. Together they make the G3.5 tier transparent to inference workloads.
beats.speaker: Presenter

slide.title: ICMS Software Architecture
slide.subtitle: Four components orchestrate KV cache across memory tiers

beats.text: The ecosystem response was immediate. At CES, fourteen major storage and infrastructure vendors announced support: Dell Technologies, HPE, IBM, Pure Storage, VAST Data, WEKA, DDN, Nutanix, and more. VAST Data went furthest, announcing a full redesign of its AI inference architecture for the agentic era — built ground-up on BlueField-4.
beats.speaker: Presenter

content.label: VAST Data
content.text: Announced full redesign of AI inference architecture for the agentic era — built ground-up on BlueField-4

beats.text: BlueField-4 enters early availability as part of the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform in early 2026, with general availability in the second half of the year. For teams already running BlueField applications, NVIDIA is maintaining backward compatibility — the transition is designed to be additive, not disruptive.
beats.speaker: Presenter

slide.title: BlueField-4 & ICMS Availability
slide.subtitle: Rolling out on the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform

items.title: CES Announcement
items.description: BlueField-4 and ICMS platform unveiled. 14 storage & infrastructure partners announced.

items.title: Early Availability
items.description: BlueField-4 available as part of NVIDIA Vera Rubin platforms. Backward-compatible with existing BlueField applications.

items.title: General Availability
items.description: Full GA release. ICMS platform and G3.5 storage tier broadly available for AI factory deployments.

items.title: Agentic AI Scale
items.description: G3.5 established as a permanent tier in the AI data center hierarchy as multi-million-token context agents become standard.

beats.text: BlueField-4 and ICMS mark something bigger than a product launch. NVIDIA now controls the full inference stack — from HBM inside the GPU, across the Spectrum-X network fabric, through the BlueField-4 DPU into flash storage, all orchestrated by Dynamo, DOCA, Grove, and NIXL. As agentic AI scales, this vertically integrated architecture becomes the foundation every AI factory will be built on.
beats.speaker: Presenter

content.value: NVIDIA Owns the Full Inference Stack

items.text: {success:Vertical integration} from HBM to flash to fabric

items: Standardizing the AI data center hierarchy
